authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife
USDA Forest Service (Lake Tahoe Basin Management Unit)

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(1) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Angora Lake Lower not listed)
CCR T14 §7.50 — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Angora Lake Lower not listed)
2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (Updated 06/01/2026)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and 10 trout possession limit.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon — CDFW
sources.quotedText: R2 El Dorado Angora Lake Lower Lahontan Cutthroat Trout Fingerling Put and Grow September-November

sources.title: Lahontan Cutthroat Trout | California Department of Fish and Wildlife
sources.quotedText: In California, the historic range of Lahontan cutthroat trout includes Lake Tahoe and the Carson, Truckee, and Walker river basins.

sources.title: Lake Tahoe Basin Management Unit | Angora Lakes Resort | Forest Service
sources.quotedText: Angora Lakes Resort, located on the south end of Lake Tahoe, is a popular destination during the summer months. A parking fee is required.

parameters.note: Angora Lake Lower is not listed in CCR §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it falls under the general statewide lake regulation in §5.85(a)(1).

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year (general statewide lake regulation; not listed as a special-regulation water)
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.counting_rule: General statewide trout limit for inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds: five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit, 10 trout possession limit
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

parameters.description: Angora Lake Lower sits in the USFS Angora Lakes recreation area (Lake Tahoe Basin Management Unit), reached on foot from the Angora Ridge Road parking area; a parking fee is required.

regulations.citation: USFS Lake Tahoe Basin Management Unit — Angora Lakes Resort (Overview)
regulations.humanSummary: Walk-in access from the USFS Angora Lakes parking area; a parking fee is required
regulations.verbatimText: Angora Lakes Resort, located on the south end of Lake Tahoe, is a popular destination during the summer months. A parking fee is required.

stockingSchedule.note: CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon lists Angora Lake Lower (Region 2, El Dorado Co.) for fingerling-size Lahontan Cutthroat Trout under the Put and Grow program, with a stated release month range of September-November. This is CDFW's provisional plan for 2026, not a guaranteed annual constant.
